# Dependency Map — Form 1040 Line 15 (Taxable Income)

**Tax Year:** 2025
**Last updated:** 2026-06-14 (re-authored against `lines/15.md`)

Line 15 is **taxable income**: AGI (line 11b) minus total deductions (line 14), floored at zero. Per spec `lines/15.md` §1:

```
line15 = max(0, line11b − line14)
```

Where `line14 = line12e + line13a + line13b`. Line 15 is the core branch input for line 16 tax computation (Tax Table, TCW, QDCG, Schedule D Tax Worksheet, Form 8615, FEITW). The zero case at line 15 acts as a **branch gate**: the Foreign Earned Income Tax Worksheet (FEITW) must NOT be completed when line 15 = 0 (spec §6.3 + IRC §911).

---

## 1. Computation identity

| Item | Value |
|---|---|
| Form 1040 label | Line 15 — "Subtract line 14 from line 11b. If zero or less, enter -0-. This is your taxable income." |
| Formula | `line15 = roundMoney(subtractNonNegative(line11b, line14))` |
| Backend orchestrator | **NONE** — line 15 is inline-computed at 5 wiring sites |
| Output fields | `form1040.deductions.line15TaxableIncome` (canonical), `form1040.deductions.taxableIncome` (legacy alias) |
| Output type | `BigDecimal`, whole-dollar HALF_UP rounded |
| Floor | **MANDATORY zero-floor** — structurally enforced by `subtractNonNegative(...)` |

---

## 2. Inputs

### 2.1 Direct inputs

| Input | Source | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| `line11b` (AGI carryforward) | `computeAdjustments()` | `form1040.adjustments.line11bAmountFromLine11aAdjustedGrossIncome`. May be null. Negative values pass through (no floor at line 11a/11b) but zero-floor at line 15 publishes 0. |
| `line14` (total deductions) | Inline-assembled at 4 sites (see `dependencies/14.md`) | `line12e + (line13a ?? 0) + (line13b ?? 0)`. May be null at very early sites, but is non-null by Site γ/δ when authoritative line 15 is published. |

### 2.2 Line 14 components

| Component | Source | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| `line12e` | `computeLine12()` | Standard or itemized deduction. Always ≥ 0 (standard deduction floor ensures > 0 unless line 12b/12c hard-zero per IRC §63(c)(6)). |
| `line13a` | `computeLine13a()` second-pass | QBI deduction (Form 8995 / 8995-A). Null when QBI blocked or absent. |
| `line13b` | `computeSchedule1A()` | Schedule 1-A line 38 (tips + overtime + car loan + senior). Null/0 when no additional deductions. |

### 2.3 Inputs that do NOT feed line 15 directly

Do **not** feed line 15 directly from Schedule A alone, Form 8995/8995-A alone, Schedule 1-A alone, line 11a without first carrying to line 11b, or line 12e alone. Those items must flow through the actual Form 1040 line structure first.

---

## 3. Computation

```text
subtractNonNegative(line11b, line14):
  if line11b == null → return null
  diff = line11b − (line14 ?? 0)
  return max(0, diff)

line15 = roundMoney(subtractNonNegative(line11b, line14))
```

### Zero-floor (structural)

```
if line11b − line14 <= 0:
    line15 = 0
```

Different from lines 11a and 11b, which do NOT have a zero-floor. All five line-15 wiring sites use the helper `subtractNonNegative(...)` which returns 0 when the result is negative — the zero-floor is therefore **structurally enforced**, not validated at runtime.

---

## 4. Wiring sites (5 sites — no orchestrator)

Line 15 is inline-computed at 5 wiring sites during `prepare()`. Sites α / β / γ / δ pair with line 14 Sites A / B / C / D; Site ε is unique to line 15.

| Site | Location (`TaxReturnComputeService.java`) | Computation | Pairs with line 14 |
|---|---|---|---|
| **Site α** — first-pass (`computeLine12`) | ~line 5706 | `subtractNonNegative(agi, line14_preliminary)` | Line 14 Site A |
| **Site β** — Schedule 1-A wiring (`prepare()`) | ~line 1228 | `subtractNonNegative(agi, line14_interim)` | Line 14 Site B |
| **Site γ** — second-pass SUCCESS (`prepare()`) | ~line 1431 | `subtractNonNegative(agiFinal, newLine14)` | Line 14 Site C |
| **Site δ** — second-pass ELSE degenerate (`prepare()`) | ~line 1517 | `subtractNonNegative(agiFinal, newLine14)` | Line 14 Site D |
| **Site ε** — Form 8396 Schedule A reduction recompute | ~line 23654 | `subtractNonNegative(agi, recomputed_line14)` | (unique — no direct line 14 audit pair) |

**Site ε** is a rare recompute that fires only when Form 8396 retroactively reduces the Schedule A mortgage interest deduction (mortgage credit certificate). The reduction propagates: smaller itemized deduction → smaller line 14 → larger line 15. This is the only case where line 15 changes after `computeLine16` would otherwise run; Form 8396 lives upstream of the final tax-credit pass and the recompute keeps line 15 consistent.

**Misleading local variable name at Site β**: the assignment `BigDecimal line14Final = roundMoney(subtractNonNegative(agi13b, ...))` at ~line 1228 actually holds the **line 15 value**, not line 14. The setters at the next two lines (`setTaxableIncome` + `setLine15TaxableIncome`) correctly persist the value, so the bug is purely cosmetic. A clearer name would be `line15Interim`. Refactor deferred per anti-fragmentation policy (15 #6 + 15 #9).

---

## 5. Outputs

| Field | JSON path | IRS form | Notes |
|---|---|---|---|
| `line15TaxableIncome` | `form1040.deductions.line15TaxableIncome` | Form 1040 line 15 | Canonical explicit field |
| `taxableIncome` | `form1040.deductions.taxableIncome` | — | Legacy alias — same value (kept for frontend compat) |

---

## 6. PDF fields

| CSV label | PDF field | Page | Content |
|---|---|---|---|
| `line15_taxable_income` | `f2_06[0]` | 2 | Line 15 taxable income |

Frontend fills this from `form.deductions.taxableIncome` (legacy field).

---

## 7. Downstream consumers

| Consumer | How used |
|---|---|
| `computeLine16()` | ★★ PRIMARY — gates on null (skip) and zero (Branch 1 ZERO method); drives Tax Table / TCW / QDCG / Schedule D TW / Form 8615 / FEITW selection |
| Tax Table (Branch 6) | Applies when line 15 < $100,000 (label-only boundary) |
| Tax Computation Worksheet (Branch 7) | Applies when line 15 ≥ $100,000 (label-only boundary) |
| QDCG Worksheet (Branch 5) | Applies when qualified dividends or capital gains present |
| Schedule D Tax Worksheet (Branch 4) | Applies when Schedule D line 18 or 19 > 0 AND lines 15+16 are gains |
| Form 8615 (Branch 3 — kiddie tax) | Child's `line15` used in the manual `childFinalTaxLine18` entry |
| Foreign Earned Income Tax Worksheet (Branch 2) | Gates on `line15 > 0` before proceeding (spec §6.3 FEITW zero gate) |
| Form 6251 (AMT) | Part I — AMTI starts from line 15 |
| Form 8880 (Saver's Credit) | Indirect via line 15 → credit ceiling |
| EIC computation | Indirect via line 15 → phaseout reference |
| Schedule J (income averaging) | **Out of scope** — farmers/fishermen (Schedule C/F blocked upstream) |

---

## 8. Blocking flags

Line 15 itself emits **no** `TaxReturnFlag`. If line 15 is null because AGI is null, `computeLine16` is silently gated and skips. Missing data flags are emitted upstream (e.g., `MISSING_W2_EMPLOYMENT_INCOME_TAXPAYER`).

Spec §7 invariants (`LINE14_MISMATCH`, `LINE15_ZERO_FLOOR_REQUIRED`, `LINE15_CANNOT_BE_NEGATIVE`) are **structurally enforced** by `subtractNonNegative` and by line 14's 4-site build-up — they would only fire if there were a bug elsewhere.

---

## 9. Compute order

```text
Line 9 (buildIncome)
  → Line 10 (computeIncomeAdjustments)
    → Lines 11a/11b (computeAdjustments)
      → Line 12e (computeLine12)
        → Line 13a first-pass (preliminary; line13b = null)
          → Line 14 Site A + Line 15 Site α (PRELIMINARY)
            → Line 13b (computeSchedule1A)
              → Line 14 Site B + Line 15 Site β (INTERIM AUTHORITATIVE)
                → Line 13a second pass (authoritative; uses line13b)
                  ├─ SUCCESS: Line 14 Site C + Line 15 Site γ (FINAL)
                  └─ ELSE:    Line 14 Site D + Line 15 Site δ (FINAL when QBI blocked)
                    → Line 16 (computeLine16)
                      → ...
                      → (rare) Form 8396 recompute → Line 15 Site ε
```

**Important**: 13b must finalize before 13a's authoritative pass because the QBI deduction's taxable-income base is `line11a − line12e − line13b`. Therefore line 15 must NOT be computed authoritatively until the full `11b / 12e / 13a / 13b / 14` chain is stable (Sites γ or δ).

---

## 10. MFS protection (transitive)

Line 15 has **no MFS orchestrator guard** of its own — it is inline-computed at 5 sites and inherits MFS protection transitively from its 2 direct inputs:

- **line 11b** via `computeAdjustments` — itself inheriting from 14 income-territory orchestrators (the income lines 1c through 8 cluster) plus `computeIncomeAdjustments` per the line 10 #1 cascade;
- **line 14** via line 12e (12abcde cluster — 15th orchestrator) + line 13a (16th) + line 13b (17th).

Both inputs are MFS-correct before they feed line 15; the subtraction + zero-floor cannot introduce new MFS leakage. Site ε at Form 8396 also operates on MFS-guarded Schedule A inputs.

Per 15 #1 — 9th defensive-gap-NOT-needed Issue #1 in the audit workflow.

---

## 11. Personal forms (none direct)

Line 15 is a pure computation line. No personal intake form provides direct input to it. All data enters via lines 11b and 14.

---

## 12. Statement types (none direct)

No statement type provides direct input to line 15. Statements affect earlier income and deduction lines that eventually flow through lines 11b and 14 to reach line 15.

---

## 13. Special cases

| Case | Result |
|---|---|
| `line11b == null` | `line15TaxableIncome = null`; line 16 skipped |
| `line11b − line14 ≤ 0` | `line15TaxableIncome = 0`; `regularTax = 0` (Branch 1 ZERO); FEITW is structurally blocked (spec §6.3) |
| `line11b > 0`, no adjustments, no deductions | `line15TaxableIncome = line11b` (rare) |
| `line15 = 0` + Box 1/2/3 add-on present | Line 16 may still be > 0 (per spec `lines/16.md` §2.3); Branch 1 makes `regularTax = 0` but add-ons stack on top |
| Form 8396 retroactive Schedule A reduction | Site ε recomputes line 15 (larger taxable income) after `computeLine16` would otherwise run |

---
